questions.text: Define the term "Constitution" and explain its significance in the Indian context.
Explain the role of the Preamble in serving as the guiding spirit of the Constitution.
Define the term "State" in the Constitutional context. List and briefly explain any two fundamental rights granted to Indian citizens.
What protection are available to the Indian Citizen against conviction?
What are the Constitutional provisions and procedures outlined in the Indian Constitution for the impeachment of the President of India?
Discuss the circumstances under which Supreme Court of India may grant Special Leave to Appeal.
Discuss the appointment and removal of Governor.
Suppose a state legislature passes a law that is potentially in violation of fundamental rights. Analyse the legal avenues available to challenge such legislation.
Differentiate between Union list, State list and Concurrent list.
Evaluate the role of Comptroller and Auditor General in ensuring financial accountability.
Provide a comprehensive overview of the historical background leading to the adoption of the Constitution of India.
List and explain the salient features of the Constitution of India.
Explain the concept of citizenship in India. Discuss the provisions for acquiring and losing citizenship according to the Constitution.
Analyse a hypothetical scenario where a new territory seeks to join the Indian Union. Discuss the Constitutional provisions and processes involved.
Differentiate between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles of state Policy. Provide examples of each.
Explain Right against exploitation and Right to Constitutional Remedies.
Discuss the different groups under which Directive Principles of State Policy can be categorized.
Enumerate the fundamental duties and discuss their significance.
Provide an in-depth overview of the Union executive, emphasizing the roles of President and Prime minister.
Discuss the qualifications, election process, term of office and powers and functions of the Vice President as outlined in the Indian Constitution.
Discuss the provisions in the Indian constitution regarding the composition and functioning of the Parliament.
